skill.name: hyperframes-cli
skill.description: Use the HyperFrames CLI to scaffold, lint, inspect, preview, render, transcribe, generate speech, and troubleshoot HyperFrames video projects.
skill.summary: Run HyperFrames CLI workflows for video project setup, validation, preview, rendering, and media utilities.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static backtick detections are Markdown command examples, not Ruby or shell execution inside the skill file. The skill is still medium risk because it instructs agents to run npx hyperframes commands that can execute package code, write files, launch browser or FFmpeg workflows, and process local media. The weak cryptographic algorithm alert on SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive; no cryptographic operation or malicious prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Through npx
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs agents to run npx hyperframes commands. This is expected for a CLI skill, but npx can execute package code and the commands may start browser, rendering, transcription, TTS, and diagnostic workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and repeated throughout the skill file. They appear legitimate for the stated purpose, so the risk is execution side effects rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Local File and Media Processing Side Effects
medium_findings.description: The skill describes commands that create project structure, copy media, lint project files, render output files, transcribe media, and generate speech files. These actions can modify workspace content and process user media.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly documents write and media-processing behavior. The behavior is normal for video tooling, but it requires workspace scoping and user awareness.

low_findings.title: Localhost Preview URL Is Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are localhost preview examples for the HyperFrames studio. No evidence found that the skill sends data to a remote endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URL examples use localhost and are described as preview links. This strongly supports a false positive for external network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The static high-risk crypto alert points to description metadata. No evidence found of a cryptographic algorithm, hashing routine, encryption routine, or credential handling in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive frontmatter for the CLI skill. The file contains no crypto implementation or security-sensitive cryptographic choice.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Directed External Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to run npx hyperframes across setup, linting, inspection, preview, rendering, transcription, TTS, diagnostics, and benchmarking. Users should approve command execution and understand workspace impact.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is directly observable and central to the skill. It is not malicious by itself, but it is a meaningful marketplace risk pattern.

content.user_title: Build HyperFrames Videos with the CLI
content.value_statement: Video projects need repeatable setup, validation, preview, and rendering steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the HyperFrames CLI workflow.
actual_capabilities: Scaffolds HyperFrames projects with blank, example, video, audio, and non-interactive options.
Runs lint checks for composition structure, timeline registration, and required data attributes.
Inspects rendered layouts for text overflow, clipping, and canvas boundary problems.
Starts a local preview studio and formats the correct project preview URL.
Renders MP4 or WebM outputs with quality, frame rate, worker, Docker, GPU, and strictness options.
Supports transcription, text-to-speech, environment diagnostics, browser management, documentation, and benchmarking commands.
limitations: Requires Node.js 22 or later and FFmpeg before core workflows can run.
Executes an external npm CLI through npx, so users should approve commands before use.
Rendering can launch Chrome and consume significant CPU, memory, and disk space.
The skill documents CLI usage but does not include the HyperFrames package source code.

use_cases.title: Start a New Video Composition
use_cases.description: Create a HyperFrames project from a template or imported media, then prepare it for editing.

use_cases.title: Validate Layout Before Review
use_cases.description: Run lint and visual inspection to catch missing composition metadata, clipped text, and overflow problems.

use_cases.title: Render and Diagnose Delivery Builds
use_cases.description: Produce review or final video files, then use doctor, info, and benchmark commands when rendering fails.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold a Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new HyperFrames project named my-video with an appropriate starter template. Explain each command before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the HyperFrames lint and inspect workflow for this project. Summarize errors, warnings, and required fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Preview and Share URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Start the HyperFrames preview studio on an available port. Give me the studio project URL, not the source file path.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Final Render
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the project, choose final render settings, render the video, and report any environment or layout risks before delivery.

output_examples.input: Inspect a project with captions and speech bubbles.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs lint, runs visual inspection, then reports which captions overflow containers and which warnings are safe to review manually.
The assistant verifies the environment, selects high quality settings, renders the output file, and summarizes the produced video location and render warnings.

output_examples.input: Create narration from a script file.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks available voices, generates the speech file, and confirms where the narration asset was saved.

best_practices: Run lint and visual inspection before previewing or rendering a project.
Use draft quality while iterating and high quality only for final delivery.
Confirm the preview port and report the studio project URL to users.
anti_patterns: Do not run npx commands without user awareness in sensitive workspaces.
Do not hand back index.html as the preview surface for a HyperFrames project.
Do not skip doctor checks when rendering fails because environment issues are common.

faq.question: What does this skill help automate?
faq.answer: It guides HyperFrames CLI workflows for project setup, linting, inspection, preview, rendering, transcription, speech generation, and diagnostics.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is built around npx hyperframes commands, so command execution should be reviewed before use.

faq.question: Can it create or modify files?
faq.answer: Yes. Scaffolding, rendering, transcription, and text-to-speech commands can create project, media, and output files.

faq.question: Does it require external software?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow requires Node.js 22 or later and FFmpeg, and rendering can use Chrome.

faq.question: Is the localhost URL a remote network risk?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The documented URLs are local preview studio links for browser review.

faq.question: When should I use strict mode?
faq.answer: Use strict mode when a render or validation step should fail on layout warnings, not only errors.
